feedback: I have been facing persistent network coverage issues in my residential area for over two months now. Despite raising three separate complaint tickets and speaking with customer support each time, there has been zero improvement. Calls drop constantly and the internet speed is unusable during evening hours when everyone in the colony is online.

The billing department has overcharged me for three consecutive months and each time I call customer support they promise a credit that never materializes on my next statement. I have spent cumulative hours on hold waiting for agents who ultimately cannot resolve a simple billing error. This level of incompetence is staggering for a major carrier.

After switching to this carrier last month the overall experience has been average. The internet speed is adequate for most tasks, the network coverage is present in areas where my previous provider struggled, and the recharge plan pricing is in line with what competitors charge in the market.

plz fix the intrenet speed in my area. been suffering for weeks with barely 2mbps on a plan that promises 50mbps. raised complaint multiple times but custmer care just keeps saying they r working on it. no actual resolution provided ever. very frustrating experience

The 5G experience in my city has exceeded all expectations. I consistently get download speeds above 700 Mbps and the latency is low enough for competitive online gaming. The network coverage for 5G extends across most areas I visit daily and the value for money on the premium plan is justified by this performance.

omg worst experience with sim activation ever. submitted documents a week ago and the status still shows processing. called custmer support 4 times and each agent gives me a different timeline. one said 24 hrs another said 5 days. nobody actually knows whats happening with my application

I want to express my genuine satisfaction with the customer support team who handled my complex roaming issue yesterday. The agent stayed on the line for thirty minutes patiently resolving a billing dispute from my international trip. She also proactively suggested a better roaming pack for my upcoming travel. Exemplary service.

The data balance on my account has been draining at an alarming rate that does not correspond with my actual usage patterns. I have monitored my phone carefully and background data is restricted yet the balance depletes twice as fast as it should. Customer support dismisses my concern every time I raise it.

I have been evaluating the value for money on my current recharge plan and it is acceptable for what I receive. The internet speed is adequate for basic needs, the OTT bundle covers mainstream platforms, and the data validity of 84 days is a standard offering. Nothing stands out positively or negatively.

Pleasantly surprised by the call quality improvement in my neighborhood since last month. Previously every call had static and drops but now conversations are crystal clear throughout. Network coverage bars show full and actual performance matches. Whatever tower maintenance they did has made a massive difference.

The mobile app experience is functional since the last update. Previously it would crash during recharges but that is fixed now. The data balance display works and the plan comparison feature exists. It does what it needs to do without being particularly impressive or problematic.

I purchased the international roaming pack for my business trip to Europe but it simply did not activate upon landing despite multiple attempts through the app. Spent two days abroad without any connectivity until I found WiFi to contact customer support who said it was a provisioning error. Completely unacceptable.

After comparing with three other carriers I can say this network offers a standard balance of pricing, internet speed, and network coverage for users in my metro city. The recharge plan at 599 provides average benefits and the billing process functions as expected without issues.

v bad experience with the reacharg plans lately. they keep reducing data allocation while maintaining the same price. my 299 plan used to give 2gb daily now its only 1.5gb. data validity also shortened from 28 to 24 days. how is this acceptable. zero value for money anymore

The network coverage and call quality in my rural area have been consistent over the past six months. I get reception inside my house and calls go through without issues. Not dramatically better than before but functional and reliable for my daily needs in this location.

ngl disappointed with the OTT bundle that comes with my recharge plan. They advertise Netflix and Prime but what you actually get is the lowest mobile-only tier with 480p streaming. For a plan costing 599 rupees I expected at least HD quality. Feels like false advertising to attract customers.

The customer support experience on the priority line has been consistently excellent throughout my two years as a premium subscriber. Agents are knowledgeable, wait times are minimal, and issues get resolved on the first call. This alone justifies the higher pricing of the premium plan for me.

The internet speed on this carrier has been embarrassingly slow for the entire quarter in my locality. Multiple neighbors confirm the same issue suggesting it is a tower or infrastructure problem rather than individual device issues. We have collectively written to the company but received only template responses with no action.

I am thoroughly impressed with the eSIM activation process they have implemented. Scanned the QR code, completed video KYC in three minutes, and had 5G connectivity within fifteen minutes of starting. The mobile app guided me through every step clearly. This is what digital onboarding should look like.

The pricing structure on their recharge plans has become increasingly predatory. Every quarter they reduce benefits while maintaining or increasing prices. The data validity keeps shrinking and the value for money has completely eroded compared to when I first joined two years ago. Loyalty means nothing here.

The billing system on this carrier has been consistent throughout my three years of service. Every charge is itemized, there are no unexpected fees, and the auto-debit works every cycle. Standard process that functions as expected without any notable issues or surprises.

prblm with my data balance is that it drains overnight even when my phone is on airplane mode. how is that possible. i tracked it for a week and every morning 200-300mb is gone without any usage. custmer care says its system updates but i disabled all auto updates. clearly a system error on their end

The combination of fast 5G internet speed, comprehensive OTT bundle, and competitive pricing on the 799 plan makes this carrier my top choice. I stream in 4K daily without buffering and the data allocation is generous enough that I never worry about running out before the validity period ends.

The roaming service was a complete disaster during my recent trip to Southeast Asia. Despite activating the international pack three days before departure the service never connected to partner networks abroad. Customer support suggested manually selecting networks which did not work either. Ended up buying a local SIM.

Since moving to the new city the network coverage here is adequate. Signal strength is present in most areas including underground metro stations. Call quality is clear and internet speed is consistent throughout the day. Standard connectivity experience for a metro city.

tbh ok experience with the carrier overall but the data validity issue really bothers me. paying the same amount every month but now getting 4 fewer days of service compared to last year. combined with reduced data per day it means significantly less total data for identical pricing. not happy about that trend

The annual recharge plan has been outstanding value for my family. At 2999 rupees for 365 days we get generous daily data, unlimited calls, and full OTT access. The billing is just once a year which eliminates monthly recharge hassle. For households on a budget this is the most economical option available.

ur mobile app is completely broken on ios. crashes when i try to reacharg, shows wrong data balance, and the ott bundle activation links lead to error pages. been like this for 2 weeks since the last update. plz fix this immediately. i shouldnt have to use the website for basic account management

I want to note that network coverage in our industrial area has been stable this past quarter. The signal is present on most factory floors and basic calls go through without major issues. Standard coverage that meets minimum requirements for workplace communication.

The customer support interaction I had yesterday was beyond frustrating. Called about a simple billing query and was transferred between four departments over ninety minutes. Each agent asked me to verify my identity from scratch and none had access to notes from previous agents. A basic question took half a day to resolve.

I have been monitoring my internet speed for the past month and it delivers close to what was advertised in my plan. The 5G connection shows reasonable speeds during most hours with some expected variance between peak and off-peak periods. Acceptable performance for the price point.

The SIM activation process through number portability completed within the stated 72 hours timeframe as promised. The mobile app provided status updates at each step along the way which was informative and reduced my anxiety about the transition. Standard process that worked as described without any unexpected complications or delays.

ngl the pricing on roaming packs is outrageous compared to what international carriers offer for similar coverage. paid 499 per day in europe and got barely usable speeds that could not even load google maps properly. for that price i expect at least basic functionality. the value for money is non-existent for travelers

The data balance tracking feature in the mobile app functions as expected for daily monitoring needs. It shows consumption breakdown by application category and updates periodically throughout the day. The interface is functional and provides enough detail for me to understand my usage patterns. Standard feature that works reliably.

I am pleased to report that the persistent call quality issues in my area have been resolved after the tower maintenance last week. Conversations are now clear without the tin-can echo effect. My colleagues on the same network confirm the improvement. Credit to the technical team for finally addressing it.

The new family recharge plan has been a wonderful addition for our household. Four connections on a single account with shared OTT access, individual data balances for each member, and consolidated billing that makes expense tracking straightforward. The per-person cost works out significantly cheaper than individual plans.

intrenet speed drops to absolutely nothing after 8pm every single day without fail. this has been happening for 6 weeks straight. clearly the tower in my area is overloaded and they refuse to upgrade it. paying premium pricing for what amounts to a 2G experience during the hours i actually need connectivity the most

The billing process for postpaid plans is straightforward on this carrier. Charges are listed clearly, due date notifications come through the app, and payment works through standard channels. No hidden fees observed but also nothing that distinguishes it from other carriers.

The OTT bundle they advertise as a key selling point of the premium plan is essentially worthless. You get mobile-only tiers that cannot be cast to television, the quality is capped at standard definition, and the subscriptions randomly deactivate every few weeks requiring manual re-authentication. Complete waste of a feature.

The value for money on the quarterly plan continues to impress me each cycle. At 699 rupees for 84 days I receive 2GB daily data with rollover, unlimited calls, full OTT access on three platforms, and consistent 5G speeds in my area. When I calculate the per-day cost it is genuinely the best deal in market.

v bad network coverage situation in the newly developed township near the highway. over 500 families have moved in over the past year and there is still no proper tower serving this area. calls drop constantly and internet is unusable. we have petitioned the carrier collectively but received zero response so far

I switched from a competitor specifically for this carrier's 5G coverage in my tech park and the decision has paid off enormously. My video conferences are lag-free, large file uploads happen in seconds, and the overall internet experience is dramatically superior to what I had before on 4G with the other provider.

The SIM replacement process after my phone was stolen has been nightmarishly slow. Five days to get a replacement SIM activated when competitors offer same-day replacements at stores. Meanwhile I am without phone service for critical work calls. The store staff blamed the system while customer support blamed the store.

The call quality on this network has been consistent for my regular conference calls. Audio clarity is acceptable and calls generally complete without issues. Standard performance for the pricing tier I am on. It meets basic expectations without particularly exceeding them.

omg the billing errors are becoming a monthly tradition now. this month they charged me for a roaming pack i never activated. last month it was a VAS subscription. month before that an incorrect plan upgrade. custmer care promises resolution in 48 hours but it always takes 2 weeks and multiple follow-ups

The network coverage along the Chennai-Bangalore highway is adequate for my weekly drive. I maintain connectivity for calls and basic data usage throughout most of the route. Standard coverage with occasional brief gaps in some stretches but nothing that significantly impacts my commute.

The data validity issue is my biggest pain point with this carrier. I consistently have unused data remaining when my plan expires because the 24-day validity simply is not enough for my usage pattern. I would gladly pay slightly more for 30 days. The current structure wastes my money every single cycle.

I am genuinely pleased with the mobile app overhaul they did last quarter. The new interface is modern and responsive, recharge processing is instantaneous, and the integrated customer support chat connects me to an agent within seconds. Data balance alerts are now real-time which helps me manage my daily consumption effectively.

plz look into the internet speed issue in whitefield area. multiple users in our apartment complex are facing the same problem. speeds drop to 1-2 mbps after 7pm making it impossible to work from home. we have raised this collectively through the app but the response has been generic template messages

The recharge plan comparison feature in the app is genuinely helpful for making informed decisions. I can see exactly what each plan offers in terms of data, validity, OTT access, and pricing side by side. This transparency is appreciated and helps me pick the best value for my specific usage pattern every cycle.

I have been a loyal customer for four years and the gradual erosion of value for money is impossible to ignore anymore. When I first joined the 299 plan gave 2GB daily for 28 days. Now the same price gives 1.5GB for 24 days. That is a significant reduction in total data for identical pricing over time.

The 5G rollout in my tier-2 city is progressing. Speeds are acceptable in the main commercial areas and the coverage extends to some residential zones. The tower density seems standard as I get consistent signal in areas where 5G is available. Nothing extraordinary but functional.

custmer care is absolutely hopeless for resolving technical issues. called about my 5g not working despite being in a covered area and the agent told me to restart my phone. when that didnt work she said buy a new phone. my phone supports 5g and works fine on other networks. clearly a provisioning prblm they refuse to fix

I must commend the seamless roaming experience during my three-week business trip across six Asian countries. The weekly international pack activated flawlessly, internet speeds were sufficient for video conferencing throughout, and the billing upon return was exactly as quoted with zero surprises or hidden charges.

The network coverage issue in our apartment building has persisted for an entire year now despite numerous complaints from multiple residents. The carrier acknowledges the problem exists but claims building a new tower requires regulatory approvals that they are still waiting on. Meanwhile we all suffer with unusable indoor reception daily.

Customer support sent a notification about scheduled maintenance in my area with reasonable advance notice. They specified the time window and sent confirmation when complete. Standard communication process that most carriers follow. Network was back up within the stated timeframe.

The recharge plans have become progressively worse with each revision this year. Less data per day, shorter validity periods, reduced OTT bundle quality, and the same or higher pricing. They are clearly squeezing existing customers while using promotional rates to attract new ones. Loyal users are being punished.

I have been evaluating the overall value proposition of this carrier over the past nine months and it is acceptable. The internet speed is adequate, network coverage is standard across cities I visit, and the pricing is comparable to major competitors. Average experience overall.

cant blv they charge 499 for a roaming daily pack that gives u speeds slower than 2g. tried to stream a simple audio podcast abroad and it kept buffering. for that pricing i expect at minimum basic browsing capability. the value for money on international packs is the worst in the industry by far

The OTT bundle integration with my recharge plan has been a genuine value-add for my family's entertainment needs. Having Netflix, Prime, and Hotstar included saves us nearly 500 rupees monthly compared to subscribing individually. The streaming quality is good thanks to the reliable internet speed on our connection.

I raised a formal complaint about persistent call quality issues affecting my business calls and the resolution process has been painfully slow. The technical team was supposed to visit the tower near my office two weeks ago but it still has not happened. Meanwhile every client call I make sounds distorted and unprofessional.

The data validity on the annual plan means I only recharge once a year which is convenient for scheduling purposes. The daily data allocation is standard and balance tracking through the app functions correctly. A straightforward plan that eliminates monthly recharge decisions.

ur billing system charged me twice for the same reacharg this month. called custmer care and they confirmed the duplicate charge but said refund will take 7-10 business days. why should i wait 10 days for their system error. this is my money being held for no fault of mine. unacceptable policy

The network performance during the recent heavy monsoon period was stable without any significant degradation in service quality. Call quality remained functional and clear, and internet speed was consistent with pre-monsoon levels throughout the rainy weeks. Standard infrastructure performance that met my expectations without dramatic changes in either direction.

The customer support chatbot in the mobile app is fundamentally useless for anything beyond checking balance or plan details. It cannot handle even slightly complex queries and loops the same irrelevant suggestions endlessly. Getting to a human agent requires navigating through twenty minutes of bot interaction that solves nothing.

The coverage along my daily 40-kilometer commute route through multiple zones is consistent and reliable. I pass through urban, suburban, and semi-rural areas with functional connectivity maintained throughout the entire drive. Call quality is adequate for hands-free conversations and speeds are reasonable for background streaming during the commute.

I am incredibly frustrated that the 5G service I am paying premium prices for barely works in my area despite their coverage map showing full availability. On an average day I get 5G signal for maybe two hours and the rest of the time it falls back to 4G. The coverage map is clearly misleading customers into expensive plans.

The overall mobile app experience continues to be a strong point for this carrier. From plan management to bill payment to OTT access to customer support chat, everything is unified in one well-designed interface. The recent addition of a data usage heatmap showing which apps consume most data has been particularly enlightening.

The internet speed degradation in my locality over the past quarter has been dramatic and unaddressed. What used to be reliable 80 Mbps has dwindled to 10-15 Mbps during most of the day. I suspect tower overloading as many new apartments have come up nearby but the carrier has not scaled infrastructure to match demand.

As a frequent international traveler I can confirm that this carrier's roaming service has improved significantly over the past year. Automatic network connection upon landing, reasonable daily pack pricing compared to competitors, and decent internet speeds for productivity tasks abroad. The app makes pack activation and usage monitoring effortless.

The sim activation failure has cost me an entire week of productivity. I ported my number expecting a 72-hour transition as promised but the process stalled without explanation. My old SIM was already deactivated so I had no phone service whatsoever. Customer support offered no workaround and just kept saying please wait.

I have nothing but praise for the consistency of the call quality on this network. Over twelve months and thousands of calls I can count on one hand the number of times I experienced any audio issue. For someone who conducts business entirely over phone this kind of reliability is worth every rupee of the premium pricing.

The mobile app payment processing is unreliable and has failed me multiple times at critical moments. Twice this month a recharge transaction showed as failed in the app but the money was debited from my bank account. Getting refunds through customer support took over a week each time with multiple follow-ups needed.

The pricing on the 5G premium plan is justified by the actual speeds and coverage I receive in my metro area. Unlike competitors who charge similar rates but deliver inconsistent 5G, this carrier maintains reliable high-speed connectivity throughout my daily routine. The included OTT bundle adds genuine extra value.

I am lodging this complaint regarding the persistent data balance inaccuracies that have plagued my account for three consecutive billing cycles. My actual usage as measured by my phone's native tracking shows significantly less consumption than what the carrier reports. This discrepancy costs me money every month and nobody takes it seriously.

The self-service capabilities in the mobile app have made customer support calls almost unnecessary for me. I can troubleshoot basic network issues through guided diagnostics, manage my recharge plan changes, track my data balance in real-time, and even generate billing statements independently. Empowering digital experience.

The call quality during this monsoon season has been remarkably reliable compared to previous years. No crackling noise, no disconnections, and consistent ability to place outgoing calls regardless of weather. They clearly invested in weatherproofing their infrastructure and the improvement is noticeable and appreciated.

I recently compared my overall costs and benefits against the top three competitors and this carrier still offers the best value for money in my usage segment. The combination of reliable 5G speeds, comprehensive OTT access, and reasonable pricing creates a package that no single competitor matches across all dimensions simultaneously.

The network coverage inside metro stations and tunnels has been a persistent problem that they seem to have no interest in solving. Every day during my 45-minute underground commute I lose all connectivity which means dropped calls, failed message deliveries, and inability to stream or browse. Other carriers seem to manage this fine.

I want to appreciate the genuine value for money the 84-day recharge plan provides. At 699 rupees with 2GB daily data, unlimited calls, three OTT platforms, and consistent network performance it represents the most economical choice for my moderate usage pattern. Billing is clean and data balance tracking is accurate.

The customer support experience has improved significantly over the past few months. Wait times have decreased noticeably, agent knowledge has improved, and first-call resolution is becoming the norm again. Clearly they invested in training and hiring because the quality of interactions now feels professional and efficient.

I have been using this carrier for six months and the experience has been consistently average across all touchpoints. Network coverage is present in my routine areas, internet speed is adequate, the app functions normally, and billing processes correctly. Standard carrier experience.

ur data validity policy is ridiculous. who benefits from 24 day plans instead of proper monthly 30 day cycles. it means i have to recharge at random dates every month which is confusing and wastes leftover data. plz just standardize to 30 days like any sensible carrier would. this isnt hard to fix

The 5G experience at the airport and surrounding business district is genuinely world-class. Speed tests consistently show over 900 Mbps and the low latency makes real-time collaboration tools work flawlessly. Downloaded a complete three-hour movie in under a minute while waiting for my flight. Technology working as intended.

I find it unacceptable that the OTT bundle randomly deactivates every time my recharge renews requiring manual re-authentication on every streaming platform. This tedious process takes thirty minutes each cycle and customer support says it is by design. For a feature marketed as seamless and automatic this is anything but.

The SIM activation for my new secondary number was completed in under four hours from online order to active connection. The app provided clear progress tracking, the video KYC process was smooth, and I received a confirmation the moment my number went live. Efficient and modern digital onboarding process.

The pricing revision they implemented last month is the final straw for many long-term customers including myself. Every popular plan saw a 15-20 percent price increase with no corresponding improvement in benefits. Internet speed remains the same, data validity was not extended, and OTT bundle was actually reduced. Pure extraction.

I continue to be impressed by the call quality on this network even during heavy rain which traditionally degrades voice services. My calls remain crystal clear regardless of weather conditions suggesting they have invested properly in modern infrastructure. Combined with strong network coverage this is a reliable daily driver.

I have been tracking my data balance carefully for two weeks and the consumption perfectly matches what my phone reports. Every megabyte is accounted for accurately with no discrepancies between their system and my device. The real-time tracking in the app makes it easy to verify throughout the day.

The overall value proposition of this carrier becomes clear when you look at what competitors charge for equivalent service levels. My plan gives me reliable 5G coverage, excellent call quality, generous data limits, and included streaming services at a price point that is ten to fifteen percent below the closest alternative.

I have completely lost faith in the customer support system after my latest interaction. Reported a network outage affecting my entire street and was told it would be resolved in 4 hours. It took 3 days. No proactive update was provided and each time I called for status the agents had no information on the progress.

The internet speed on this carrier has been consistent and reliable for my work-from-home setup throughout the past quarter. Multiple devices connect simultaneously without dramatic slowdowns. Streaming, video calls, and large file transfers all function adequately. Standard broadband-equivalent performance that meets all my daily remote work requirements without particular distinction.

The mobile app UX for activating roaming packs is needlessly complicated requiring seven taps and two confirmation screens to complete what should be a one-step process. I almost missed activating before my flight because the final confirmation page kept timing out. For a travel-essential feature this friction is unacceptable design.

The billing practices on this carrier are standard and transparent. Charges are listed clearly, amounts match what I expect based on my plan, and payment processing works normally. Average billing experience consistent with industry norms without notable positives or negatives.

The internet speed throttling after daily limit is so aggressive that the connection becomes completely unusable. At 64 kbps even loading a basic text email takes forever. This punitive approach to post-limit speed is outdated when competitors offer at least 1 Mbps after limit which is enough for messaging and light browsing.

I am writing to acknowledge the outstanding support I received from the technical team regarding persistent 5G connectivity issues in my locality. After the initial complaint they sent a field engineer within 48 hours who identified a tower configuration error. The fix was deployed that same week and my speeds have been perfect since.

The network coverage inside my office building has improved significantly after they installed a small cell in our complex last month. I now get strong signal on every floor including the basement conference room. Call quality is consistently excellent which was previously my biggest frustration.

The complete package this carrier offers for the premium segment is genuinely compelling. Fast and reliable 5G internet, nationwide network coverage without dead zones in any city I visit, pristine call quality, fair billing, and an app that puts full account control in my pocket. Worth every rupee of the 999 monthly plan.

I am deeply dissatisfied with how this carrier handles the transition between recharge plan validities. There is a gap of several hours between expiry and new plan activation where I have zero service. No calls, no data, nothing. Customer support says this is normal system behavior but losing service for hours every month is not acceptable.

I want to highlight how the carrier's investment in infrastructure has transformed my daily connectivity experience over the past year. The 5G tower installed near my home delivers consistent speeds above 500 Mbps, call quality is impeccable, and the OTT streaming experience is buffer-free even at peak hours. Genuine improvement.
